commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=37308238cb729e2d7cb23f46fdd77e4206258c99)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds structured doctor health checks and repair adapters for sandbox registry files, sandbox images, sandbox scope warnings, plus the stacked structured-doctor code/tests for plugin registry, shell completion, systemd linger, and related doctor flows.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence path: a repairable missing sandbox image with a declined prompt or failing build script still reaches the unconditional checked-change append in `repairSandboxImages`.
commands.review_followup: Human/contributor follow-up is needed because the PR is draft/protected/stacked, lacks real CLI behavior proof, and has a repair-accounting defect to fix before merge.

target.title: Doctor: structure sandbox repair checks
target.body:
## Goal

Convert the sandbox repair bucket to the structured doctor contract while preserving sandbox ownership boundaries.

This PR makes sandbox registry, image, and scope diagnostics first-class health checks. The main goal is to make sandbox maintenance auditable without accidentally turning operator-owned Docker images or read-only scope warnings into automatic repairs.

## Scope

| Check | Goal | Behavior |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `core/doctor/sandbox/registry-files` | Represent legacy sandbox registry migration as structured state repair. | Detect finds legacy registry files; repair runs the existing sharded migration path. |
| `core/doctor/sandbox/images` | Separate repairable default sandbox images from operator-managed custom images. | Detect reports missing images, Docker-unavailable cases, unsupported browser backends, and operator-managed custom images; repair only handles repairable default-image work. |
| `core/doctor/sandbox-scope` | Keep scope warnings visible but read-only. | Detect emits findings; no automatic mutation is offered. |

## Structured Repair Matrix

| Check | Detect / lint behavior | Repair behavior | Dry-run / effects | Detect-after / note proof |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `core/doctor/sandbox/registry-files` | Detects legacy sandbox registry files read-only. | Runs the existing sharded registry migration through the structured repair path. | State effects describe registry migration, empty-file removal, or invalid-file quarantine; no fake single-file diff. | Detect-after confirms legacy monolithic registry paths are no longer reported; migration tests assert files/locks are gone and sharded entries are readable. |
| `core/doctor/sandbox/images` | Detects missing sandbox images, Docker-unavailable cases, unsupported browser backends, and operator-managed custom images without printing. | Converted repair calls `repairSandboxImages(...)`; legacy `maybeRepairSandboxImages(...)` remains the old print wrapper. | Process effects distinguish buildable default image repairs from custom/operator-managed image skips. | Repair guidance uses shared `DoctorPrompter.note(...)`, routed by structured adapters into ordered repair notes. |
| `core/doctor/sandbox-scope` | Emits read-only scope findings. | No automatic mutation. | No repair effects or diffs because no repair is available. | Stays lintable/read-only and does not participate in repaired validation. |
| Custom image boundary | Custom base/browser images remain operator-managed findings/warnings. | Structured repair skips them with actionable guidance rather than invoking default build scripts. | Skip/operator effects stay explicit. | Focused tests lock custom image behavior and Docker-unavailable warnings. |

## Proof

| Proof | Result |
| --- | --- |
| Head | `0559f8fdc16f` on `doctor-sandbox-structured-repairs`, stacked on #84290 and #83753. |
| Focused sandbox/core/repair/lint tests | `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-sandbox.warns-sandbox-enabled-without-docker.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed, 5 files / 77 tests. |
| Core typecheck | `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo` passed. |
| Formatting / whitespace | `node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 ...` on the touched sandbox/core files and `git diff --check doctor-next-plugin-registry..HEAD` passed. |
| Autoreview | `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --parallel-tests "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-sandbox.warns-sandbox-enabled-without-docker.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot"` passed with no accepted/actionable findings after fixing the accepted test mock typing issue. |
| Earlier Crabbox proof | Direct AWS Crabbox run `run_1807262a6457` passed 5 files / 66 tests. |

## Out of Scope

- Building or pulling real Docker images during this PR’s proof.
- Treating custom sandbox images as automatically repairable.
- Changing sandbox runtime execution behavior.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Structured doctor repair conversion for sandbox registry files, sandbox image checks, and sandbox scope warnings with isomorphic `detect -> repair/dry-run -> detect` semantics and operator-managed custom image handling.

Real environment tested: WSL source worktree `/tmp/openclaw-doctor-next` for restack/focused proof, plus earlier direct AWS Crabbox lease `cbx_c86ebac61277` (`tidal-hermit`), run `run_1807262a6457`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused sandbox doctor tests, core tsgo, formatting, diff check, autoreview with parallel focused tests, and earlier Crabbox focused test retry.

Evidence after fix: Restacked head `0559f8fdc16f` passed local focused proof and autoreview. Crabbox run `run_1807262a6457` exited 0 with 5 test files / 66 tests passed. Proof artifact: https://gist.github.com/giodl73-repo/c6ff5e2d3d3d5ca04e5fd3c5fb0c660d.

Observed result after fix: structured dry-run reports sandbox registry migration effects without mutation; sandbox image dry-run reports repairable default image setup separately from operator-managed custom images; repair skips non-repairable custom images with actionable warnings; repair notes are routed through the shared note contract; sandbox scope findings stay read-only.

What was not tested: full changed gate, actual Docker image build/pull, package/update survivor lanes, and live Docker sandbox execution.
target.author: giodl73-repo
